text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Clinical Trial Cycle 4 Assessment — Phase II EGFR/MET Bispecific + SOS1 Inhibitor Study

ATTENDING: [Thoracic Oncology]
DEPARTMENT: Medical Oncology / Thoracic Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Ms. Margaret Tran is a 64-year-old Vietnamese-American female with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma presenting for cycle 4 completion assessment and ongoing clinical trial monitoring visit. She is currently enrolled on a phase II protocol investigating an investigational EGFR/MET bispecific monoclonal antibody in combination with an oral SOS1 inhibitor for EGFR-mutant NSCLC with acquired T790M resistance following third-generation TKI failure.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Tran is a 64-year-old former smoker (12 pack-years, quit ~20 years ago) with a well-established diagnosis of right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, originally staged as IIIB (T3N2M0) at age 62, harboring an EGFR exon 21 L858R activating mutation with co-mutant TP53 R175H. She was initially managed with osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as first-line targeted therapy given the multidisciplinary team decision to favor TKI over upfront chemoradiation — she had an excellent initial response lasting approximately 14 months before systemic and CNS progression. At progression, tissue re-biopsy of a new right adrenal metastasis confirmed acquired EGFR T790M as the dominant resistance mechanism, with co-occurring subclonal PIK3CA E545K and low-level CDK4 amplification. MRI brain at that time revealed three new enhancing intracranial lesions (right cerebellar 1.1 cm, left frontal 0.8 cm, right parietal 0.6 cm), which were treated with Gamma Knife SRS. She then received 4 cycles of carboplatin (initially AUC 5, reduced to AUC 4 after cycle 2 for grade 3 thrombocytopenia) plus pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 with a partial response, followed by 2 cycles of pemetrexed maintenance. She was subsequently enrolled on the current phase II clinical trial and initiated on the investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody (IV, q2 weeks) plus oral SOS1 inhibitor (daily, 28-day cycles).

Today marks her return for the cycle 4 completion/assessment visit. She has now been on study for approximately 16 weeks. She reports feeling meaningfully better compared to her nadir during platinum chemotherapy. Notably, dyspnea that was a prominent symptom at initial presentation and at the time of osimertinib progression has largely resolved. She is back to part-time teaching and describes her energy as "about 70% of normal." She reports no new neurological symptoms — no headaches, no focal weakness, no visual changes, no gait instability.

She did have a grade 2 infusion-related reaction (IRR) with her cycle 1 day 15 infusion of the bispecific antibody, characterized by flushing, urticaria, and transient hypotension responsive to diphenhydramine and hydrocortisone. Enhanced pre-medication with diphenhydramine, acetaminophen, and IV dexamethasone has been in place since cycle 2 and she has had no further IRR events. A mild EGFR-class rash re-emerged on her face and neck in the first few weeks of trial therapy, consistent with on-target EGFR inhibition; this has been well-controlled on topical clindamycin 1% gel and is currently grade 1.

The oral SOS1 inhibitor component has been associated with grade 1 nausea, managed with ondansetron PRN. She takes it with food in the morning and reports this has made a significant difference in tolerability. No dose holds or reductions have been required for either study agent.

Residual grade 1 peripheral neuropathy in the fingertips, a carry-over from prior carboplatin exposure, remains stable and has not worsened since initiating trial therapy. No new pain complaints. Appetite improved. Weight stable to slightly increased.

Bowel and bladder function normal. No hematuria or lower extremity edema.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Fatigue improved (grade 1, stable). No fevers, chills, or night sweats. Weight stable to slightly improved.
- Pulmonary: Dyspnea resolved at rest; mild exertional limitation with vigorous activity. No hemoptysis. No cough.
- Neurological: Denies headache, focal weakness, seizure activity, changes in vision, gait problems, or cognitive changes.
- Dermatologic: Mild facial/neck acneiform rash, grade 1, stable. No skin breakdown.
- GI: Grade 1 nausea, well-controlled with ondansetron. No vomiting. Normal bowel habits. No mucositis.
- Musculoskeletal: Fingertip paresthesias (bilateral, grade 1) from prior carboplatin, stable. No new bone pain.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
- Hematologic: No bleeding. No easy bruising.
- Endocrine: Hypothyroidism — on levothyroxine, reports no symptoms of under- or over-replacement.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY / ONCOLOGIC HISTORY:
- Stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma (originally staged IIIB at diagnosis, now M1c)
  - TP53 p.R175H co-mutation; MSS; TMB 6.2 mut/Mb (low)
  - Treatment history:
    1. Osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily x ~14 months — partial response, then progression with T790M resistance
    2. Gamma Knife SRS to 3 brain metastases (right cerebellum 20 Gy, left frontal 22 Gy, right parietal 24 Gy)
    3. Carboplatin AUC 5→4 / pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q21 days x 4 cycles — partial response
    4. Pemetrexed maintenance x 2 cycles — stable disease
    5. Current: Phase II trial — investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody + oral SOS1 inhibitor
- Hypertension (well-controlled on amlodipine)
- Hypothyroidism (stable on levothyroxine)
- Former smoker, 12 pack-years, quit approximately 20 years ago

ALLERGIES: NKDA. Infusion reaction history to investigational bispecific antibody (grade 2, cycle 1 day 15) — managed with enhanced pre-medication; no formal drug allergy designation.

MEDICATIONS:
- Investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody (protocol-assigned dose, IV q2 weeks)
- Oral SOS1 inhibitor (protocol-assigned dose, daily)
- Levothyroxine 88 mcg PO daily
- Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily
- Folic acid 400 mcg PO daily (continued from prior pemetrexed era; to be reassessed by protocol team)
- Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN nausea (q8h as needed)
- Topical clindamycin 1% gel to face/neck BID
- Acetaminophen 650 mg PO PRN (for pre-medication and headache)
- Diphenhydramine 25 mg PO (pre-medication before bispecific infusions)
- Dexamethasone 8 mg IV (pre-medication before bispecific infusions)

SOCIAL HISTORY: High school biology teacher, working part-time currently. Vietnamese-American, emigrated from Saigon at age 14. Married, supportive family. Former smoker, 12 pack-years, quit approximately 20 years ago. No alcohol or illicit drug use.

FAMILY HISTORY: Non-contributory for lung cancer or other malignancies per prior documentation.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 126/74 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 16, SpO2 97% on room air, Temp 98.1°F, Weight 59.2 kg (BSA ~1.67 m2)
ECOG Performance Status: 1

General: Well-appearing, Vietnamese-American female in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Conversant, in good spirits.
HEENT: Scattered grade 1 papulopustular lesions on forehead, bilateral cheeks, and neck — consistent with EGFR-class toxicity. No confluent areas, no crusting or superinfection. Oral mucosa moist, no mucositis or thrush. No lymphadenopathy appreciated in cervical or supraclavicular chains.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No peripheral edema.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing, crackles, or rhonchi. Good air movement throughout. No dullness to percussion.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. No palpable masses. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Neurological: Alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Gait normal. Coordination intact on finger-nose testing bilaterally. No pronator drift. Sensation decreased to light touch in bilateral fingertips (grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, baseline from prior carboplatin).
Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. Bilateral fingertip paresthesias as noted. Nails: residual post-inflammatory changes from prior paronychia, healed.
Skin: EGFR-class rash as described; otherwise no rashes, petechiae, or ecchymoses.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (most recent, obtained this visit):
- CBC: WBC 6.1 K/uL, Hgb 10.8 g/dL (mild anemia, stable — consistent with ongoing disease and treatment), Plt 184 K/uL (recovered from prior thrombocytopenia)
- CMP: Sodium 138, Potassium 4.1, Creatinine 0.82 mg/dL (CrCl ~72 mL/min), BUN 14, Glucose 94, Total bilirubin 0.6, AST 28, ALT 32, Albumin 3.6
- LFTs within normal limits; no hepatotoxicity signal
- TSH 2.8 (within normal range; levothyroxine dose unchanged)
- Magnesium 1.9 mg/dL (low-normal; oral supplementation to be initiated)
- Protocol-mandated correlative labs: sent per trial requirements

---

IMAGING RESULTS (reviewed this visit):
Most recent restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain were obtained after 2 cycles of trial therapy (approximately 8 weeks on study). Key findings from that imaging:
- CT chest: Right lower lobe primary mass down to 1.7 cm (from 2.3 cm at trial entry), representing continued shrinkage compared to the 3.1 cm at progression on osimertinib. Bilateral small pulmonary nodules that had been present at the time of osimertinib progression have resolved completely.
- CT abdomen: Right adrenal metastasis decreased to 0.7 cm (from 1.1 cm at trial entry and 1.4 cm at osimertinib progression).
- MRI brain: The three previously treated intracranial lesions (right cerebellum, left frontal, right parietal) continue to show treatment effect with volume reduction and central necrosis; no new intracranial lesions; no leptomeningeal enhancement.
Next protocol-mandated restaging imaging is scheduled at the 4-month mark (approximately 2 cycles hence). No imaging reviewed today.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Margaret Tran is a 64-year-old woman with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant, TP53-co-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, now on her third line of systemic therapy as part of a phase II clinical trial evaluating an investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody plus oral SOS1 inhibitor. She has completed 4 cycles and is tolerating the regimen well.

1. NSCLC — stage IV, on clinical trial, continued partial response:
   The most recent restaging imaging (after 2 cycles) demonstrated encouraging responses at all measurable sites — primary lung mass and adrenal metastasis both smaller, bilateral pulmonary nodules resolved, and intracranial lesions stable/improving post-SRS. Clinically she is doing well with improved functional status (ECOG PS 1, back to part-time work). Will proceed with cycle 5, day 1 today after confirming CBC and renal/hepatic function are acceptable (labs reviewed above — adequate). Next protocol imaging is scheduled per protocol timeline.

2. EGFR-class rash (grade 1, on-target toxicity):
   Well-controlled on topical clindamycin 1% gel BID. No dose modification needed. Patient instructed to avoid sun exposure, use gentle cleanser, and apply fragrance-free moisturizer. Will reassess at next visit.

3. Infusion-related reaction — bispecific antibody (history of grade 2, cycle 1 day 15):
   Pre-medication protocol in place (diphenhydramine 25 mg PO + acetaminophen 650 mg PO + dexamethasone 8 mg IV prior to all infusions). No IRR since enhanced pre-medication initiated. Continue current pre-medication protocol.

4. Nausea (grade 1, SOS1 inhibitor-related):
   Managed with ondansetron PRN. Instructed to take oral SOS1 inhibitor with food. Continue current management. No dose modification needed.

5. Peripheral neuropathy (grade 1, carry-over from prior carboplatin):
   Stable, not worsening since stopping platinum. Fingertip paresthesias only. No functional impairment. Will monitor. No intervention at this time.

6. Hypomagnesemia (mild, Mg 1.9):
   Initiate magnesium oxide 400 mg PO daily. Recheck magnesium at next visit. This is not uncommon with EGFR-directed therapy.

7. Anemia (mild, Hgb 10.8 g/dL, stable):
   Likely multifactorial — chronic disease, prior chemotherapy. Patient is asymptomatic from an anemia standpoint (no dyspnea at rest, no orthostasis). No transfusion indicated. Continue to monitor.

8. Hypothyroidism:
   TSH 2.8, well-controlled on levothyroxine 88 mcg daily. Continue current dose.

9. Hypertension:
   BP 126/74 — well-controlled on amlodipine 5 mg daily. Continue.

10. Correlative science / biomarker monitoring:
    Trial-mandated protocol labs including ctDNA drawn today and sent per protocol. Results to be reported to trial sponsor. Patient remains committed to the trial and the scientific mission; she expressed appreciation for the correlative blood draws given her background as a biology teacher.

FOLLOW-UP: Return in 2 weeks for cycle 5 day 15 bispecific antibody infusion. Next comprehensive restaging imaging to be scheduled at protocol-specified 4-month interval. Patient and family aware of and agreeable to the plan. All questions answered.

Time spent: approximately 50 minutes, greater than half of which was counseling and care coordination.

Electronically signed: [Attending Thoracic Oncologist]
